 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8zp","title":"fcp.terraform: State inspection and resource listing","description":"# fcp.terraform: State inspection and resource listing (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide safe state inspection so agents can:
- list resources
- show a resource
- read outputs

State contains sensitive infrastructure details; all access must be audited.

## Scope
- List resources in state.
- Show a specific resource.
- Read outputs.
- Optional: pull remote state for inspection (read-only).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.state.read` (sensitive)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile must prevent arbitrary filesystem access.
- Audit:
  - every state read emits an audit event
- NetworkConstraints default deny unless backend requires explicit egress.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `terraform.state.list`
  - `terraform.state.show`
  - `terraform.output.read`
  - Optional: `terraform.state.pull`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests with a terraform shim for:
  - correct arg formation
  - audit event emission
  - error mapping

## Acceptance criteria
- State access is safe, auditable, and bounded.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9095b","title":"[FCP3/P5.2] Implement lease, fencing, and coordinator semantics for multi node execution","description":"Background: multi node execution is unsafe without clear coordinator and fencing rules. The mesh must prevent split brain behavior where two nodes believe they own the same connector execution.

Scope: define and implement lease, fencing, and coordinator semantics in fcp-mesh, with durable records that survive process restarts and can be inspected later. Include the semantics for lease renewal, expiry, preemption, and explanation of why a node currently holds or lost authority. Also define the structured timeline events and authority records that later conformance and split brain tests must assert directly.

Acceptance: the platform has one clear story for exclusive ownership of active execution across nodes, with deterministic authority records and diagnostic traces that make split brain regressions obvious instead of mysterious.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverBasin","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:07.204606Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:58:11.210572Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:29.000468Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","fencing","leases","mesh-first","phase-5"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9095b","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ij290","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:39.131281Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2263,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9095b","author":"SilverBasin","text":"Implemented mesh-side authority inspection for lease/fencing work: added fcp-mesh AuthorityView/AuthorityRecord/AuthorityTimelineEvent, subject-bound singleton authority resolution in MeshNode planning, and direct authority assertions in the lease-contention conformance scenario.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-90y","title":"fcp.semanticscholar: Research Literature Connector","description":"## Overview
Academic research connector for Semantic Scholar API, providing semantic paper search, citation analysis, and author profiling to complement the arxiv connector.

## Core Capabilities

### Paper Search with Semantic Understanding
- Natural language query support
- Semantic similarity matching (not just keywords)
- Field-of-study filtering
- Publication date range filtering
- Venue and journal filtering
- Open access filter option

### Citation Graph and References
- Retrieve citing papers (who cited this)
- Retrieve referenced papers (bibliography)
- Citation context extraction
- Multi-hop citation traversal
- Co-citation analysis
- Bibliographic coupling detection

### Author Profiles and Metrics
- Author search by name and affiliation
- H-index and citation count retrieval
- Publication history timeline
- Co-author network mapping
- Author disambiguation
- ORCID linking where available

### Topic Classification
- Automatic topic/field assignment
- Hierarchical topic taxonomy
- Cross-disciplinary paper detection
- Emerging topic identification
- Topic trend analysis over time

### Open Access Paper Retrieval
- Identify open access versions
- Direct PDF URL when available
- Preprint linking (arxiv, bioRxiv, etc.)
- License information extraction
- Full text availability status

## Integration with Arxiv Connector
- Cross-reference arxiv IDs with S2 paper IDs
- Enrich arxiv results with citation data
- Use S2 for citation metrics, arxiv for full text
- Unified paper identifier mapping

## Implementation Notes
- Use Semantic Scholar Academic Graph API
- Respect rate limits (100 req/5min for unauthenticated)
- API key support for higher limits
- Batch request support for efficiency
- Handle partial data gracefully

## Testing Requirements
- Mock API responses for unit tests
- Rate limit handling tests
- Citation graph traversal tests
- Search relevance validation","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:50.282684625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T08:47:46.730657198Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T08:47:46.730657198Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of fcp.semanticscholar connector bead (flywheel_connectors-ixwa)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-90y","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a7vd","title":"fcp.mongodb - MongoDB document database connector","description":"## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant MongoDB connector for document CRUD/aggregation and optional change-stream streaming with strict safety and secret handling.

## Credential handling (FCP2)
- Prefer secretless connectors: use `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` and let the egress proxy inject credentials at the network boundary when possible.
- If the protocol requires credential materialization inside the connector (e.g., some DB wire protocols), secrets MUST remain in memory only, be time-bounded, never touch disk, and never appear in logs/errors.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints` (defined in the connector’s manifest/cap map bead if present).
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.
- For DB connectors, constrain raw TCP connects to explicit host:port (or Atlas endpoints) only; deny private ranges unless explicitly allowed by policy.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)

- `storage`
- `operational`
- `streaming`

> Interaction patterns (non-archetype): Database (wire protocol / Atlas Data API) + (optional) change-stream streaming

## Summary

MongoDB connector for the Flywheel Connector Platform (FCP). This document database connector enables Rust applications to interact with MongoDB clusters and Atlas deployments through type-safe operations, aggregation pipelines, and comprehensive database management capabilities.

## Context

MongoDB is the leading document-oriented NoSQL database, widely used for flexible schema designs, horizontal scaling, and real-time analytics. This connector provides:

- **Document-Oriented Model**: Native BSON document support with flexible schemas
- **Aggregation Framework**: Powerful data processing pipelines
- **Atlas Integration**: Cloud-native MongoDB Atlas Data API support
- **Change Streams**: Real-time data change notifications
- **Sharding Support**: Distributed data across multiple shards

Value Score: 51/100 (Tier 4) - Moderate adoption in Rust ecosystem, strong use case for applications requiring flexible data models.

## Archetype

**Request-Response** - Standard synchronous operations for CRUD, aggregation, and administrative commands.

## Operations

### Read Zone
- `find` - Query documents with filters and projections
- `find_one` - Retrieve single document by query
- `aggregate` - Execute aggregation pipelines
- `count_documents` - Count matching documents
- `distinct` - Get distinct values for a field
- `list_collections` - List all collections in database
- `list_databases` - List all databases on server
- `list_indexes` - List indexes on a collection
- `watch` - Open change stream cursor (streaming context)

### Write Zone
- `insert_one` - Insert single document
- `insert_many` - Bulk insert documents
- `update_one` - Update single document
- `update_many` - Update multiple documents
- `replace_one` - Replace entire document
- `delete_one` - Delete single document
- `delete_many` - Delete multiple documents
- `find_one_and_update` - Atomically find and update
- `find_one_and_replace` - Atomically find and replace
- `find_one_and_delete` - Atomically find and delete
- `bulk_write` - Execute bulk operations

### Admin Zone
- `create_collection` - Create new collection with options
- `drop_collection` - Drop a collection
- `create_index` - Create index on collection
- `drop_index` - Remove index from collection
- `run_command` - Execute raw database command
- `create_database` - Implicitly create database
- `drop_database` - Drop entire database

## Design Notes

### Architecture

```
fcp-mongodb/
├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs              # Public API exports
│   ├── client.rs           # MongoClient wrapper
│   ├── config.rs           # Configuration types
│   ├── connection.rs       # Connection pool management
│   ├── operations/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   ├── read.rs         # Find, aggregate operations
│   │   ├── write.rs        # Insert, update, delete ops
│   │   └── admin.rs        # Index, collection management
│   ├── atlas/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   └── data_api.rs     # Atlas Data API client
│   ├── pipeline/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   └── builder.rs      # Aggregation pipeline DSL
│   ├── types/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   ├── document.rs     # BSON document helpers
│   │   ├── filter.rs       # Query filter builders
│   │   └── options.rs      # Operation options
│   └── error.rs            # Error types
├── Cargo.toml
└── README.md
```

### Connection Configuration

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::{MongoConfig, MongoClient};

let config = MongoConfig::builder()
    .endpoint("mongodb+srv://cluster.mongodb.net/") // no credentials in URI
    .auth_credential_id("cred:<mongodb_auth>")      // secret reference; never stored on disk
    .database("myapp")
    .app_name("flywheel-connector")
    .min_pool_size(5)
    .max_pool_size(100)
    .connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10))
    .server_selection_timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))
    .retry_writes(true)
    .retry_reads(true)
    .build()?;

let client = MongoClient::connect(config).await?;
```

### Document Operations

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::{doc, Document};
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
struct User {
    #[serde(rename = "_id")]
    id: ObjectId,
    name: String,
    email: String,
    profile: UserProfile,
    tags: Vec<String>,
    created_at: DateTime,
}

// Type-safe operations
let users = client.collection::<User>("users");

// Insert
let user = User { /* ... */ };
let result = users.insert_one(&user).await?;

// Query with filter
let filter = doc! { "email": "user@example.com" };
let user = users.find_one(filter).await?;

// Update with operators
let update = doc! {
    "$set": { "name": "New Name" },
    "$push": { "tags": "verified" }
};
users.update_one(filter, update).await?;
```

### Aggregation Pipeline

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::pipeline::{Pipeline, stage};

let pipeline = Pipeline::new()
    .match_stage(doc! { "status": "active" })
    .lookup(stage::Lookup {
        from: "orders",
        local_field: "user_id",
        foreign_field: "customer_id",
        as_field: "orders",
    })
    .unwind("$orders")
    .group(stage::Group {
        id: "$user_id",
        fields: vec![
            ("total_orders", stage::Accumulator::Sum(1)),
            ("total_spent", stage::Accumulator::Sum("$orders.amount")),
        ],
    })
    .sort(doc! { "total_spent": -1 })
    .limit(10)
    .build();

let results = users.aggregate::<UserStats>(pipeline).await?;
```

### Index Management

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::index::{IndexModel, IndexOptions};

// Create single field index
let index = IndexModel::new(doc! { "email": 1 })
    .options(IndexOptions::builder()
        .unique(true)
        .name("email_unique")
        .build());
users.create_index(index).await?;

// Create compound index
let compound = IndexModel::new(doc! { 
    "status": 1, 
    "created_at": -1 
});
users.create_index(compound).await?;

// Create text index for search
let text_index = IndexModel::new(doc! { 
    "name": "text", 
    "description": "text" 
});
users.create_index(text_index).await?;

// Create TTL index
let ttl_index = IndexModel::new(doc! { "expires_at": 1 })
    .options(IndexOptions::builder()
        .expire_after(Duration::from_secs(0))
        .build());
sessions.create_index(ttl_index).await?;
```

### Atlas Data API Support

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::atlas::{AtlasClient, AtlasConfig};

let config = AtlasConfig::builder()
    .base_url("https://data.mongodb-api.com/app/data-xxxxx/endpoint/data/v1")
    .credential_id("cred:<atlas_data_api_key>") // secret reference; never stored on disk
    .data_source("Cluster0")
    .database("myapp")
    .build()?;

let atlas = AtlasClient::new(config);

// Operations work the same way
let users = atlas.collection::<User>("users");
let result = users.find(filter).await?;
```

### Change Streams

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::change_stream::{ChangeStream, ChangeEvent};

let mut stream = users.watch()
    .full_document(FullDocument::UpdateLookup)
    .pipeline(vec![
        doc! { "$match": { "operationType": "insert" } }
    ])
    .start().await?;

while let Some(event) = stream.next().await {
    match event?.operation_type {
        OperationType::Insert => {
            let doc = event.full_document.unwrap();
            println!("New user: {:?}", doc);
        }
        OperationType::Update => { /* ... */ }
        _ => {}
    }
}
```

### Transaction Support

```rust
let mut session = client.start_session().await?;

session.start_transaction().await?;

// Operations within transaction
let users = client.collection::<User>("users");
users.insert_one(&user).with_session(&mut session).await?;

let orders = client.collection::<Order>("orders");
orders.insert_one(&order).with_session(&mut session).await?;

session.commit_transaction().await?;
```

### Rate Limiting Integration

```rust
use fcp_ratelimit::{RateLimiter, TokenBucket};

let limiter = RateLimiter::new(TokenBucket::new(
    100,  // requests per second
    Duration::from_secs(1),
));

let client = MongoClient::builder()
    .config(config)
    .rate_limiter(limiter)
    .build().await?;
```

### Telemetry Integration

```rust
use fcp_telemetry::{Telemetry, SpanKind};

let client = MongoClient::builder()
    .config(config)
    .telemetry(Telemetry::new("fcp-mongodb"))
    .build().await?;

// Operations automatically traced
// Span: mongodb.find { db.name: "myapp", db.collection: "users", ... }
```

## Dependencies

- `fcp-core` - Core FCP traits and types
- `fcp-ratelimit` - Rate limiting for connection management
- `fcp-telemetry` - Observability and tracing
- `mongodb` - Official MongoDB Rust driver (v2.8+)
- `bson` - BSON serialization
- `serde` - Serialization framework
- `tokio` - Async runtime

## Implementation Notes

1. **Driver Choice**: Use the official `mongodb` crate as the foundation, wrapping it with FCP patterns
2. **Connection Pooling**: Leverage the driver's built-in connection pool with configurable sizes
3. **Atlas Data API**: Provide alternative HTTP-based access for serverless/edge deployments
4. **Schema Validation**: Optional JSON Schema validation on collections
5. **Retryable Operations**: Enable retryable reads/writes by default for resilience
6. **Compression**: Support zstd/snappy/zlib compression for network efficiency

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Connect to standalone, replica set, and sharded clusters
- [ ] All CRUD operations implemented with type-safe wrappers
- [ ] Aggregation pipeline builder with compile-time validation
- [ ] Index management (create, drop, list)
- [ ] Change streams for real-time updates
- [ ] Transaction support for multi-document ACID
- [ ] Atlas Data API client for serverless scenarios
- [ ] Connection pool metrics exposed via telemetry
- [ ] Rate limiting integration for throttling
- [ ] Comprehensive error types mapping driver errors
- [ ] Integration tests against MongoDB test containers
- [ ] Documentation with examples for all operations

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-c4cm","title":"[IDEA-26] Source Diversity Enforcement + Metrics","description":"## Background
Spec requires source diversity to avoid single-source reconstruction. We need mechanical enforcement and metrics to prove diversity SLOs.

## Goals
- Track per-object symbol source diversity.
- Enforce minimum distinct sources for reconstruction when policy requires.
- Emit metrics and structured logs for coverage and diversity.

## Non-goals
- Full repair controller redesign (only diversity enforcement + metrics).

## Approach
- Extend symbol store metadata to track source node IDs.
- Add policy flag for `min_source_diversity` per zone.
- Integrate with repair loop to fetch from additional sources.

## Risks
- Extra network cost; ensure policy-gated and bounded.

## Success Criteria
- Single-source prevention triggers with stable reason codes.
- Metrics expose diversity coverage per object.

fuzzable input shape:

#[derive(Arbitrary, Debug)]
struct WebhookHeaderFuzz<'a> {
    provider: WebhookProviderKind,
    signature_header: &'a [u8],

    body: &'a [u8],
}

fuzz_target!(|input: WebhookHeaderFuzz| {
    let _ = parse_provider_signature_header(input.provider, input.signature_header, input.timestamp_header);
    // Property: no panic on any input, no allocation > 16 KiB
});

properties to exercise per provider:

- Stripe: 'Stripe-Signature: t=<unix>,v1=<hex>,v0=<hex>' — fuzz with duplicated keys, mixed schemes, missing t=, t with non-numeric, multi-MB hex strings (existing 'Stripe signature unbounded allocation cap' fix per MEMORY.md should hold but coverage isn't a fuzz)
- Slack: header pair with timestamp >5 min in future, signature with leading whitespace, signature with embedded NUL
- GitHub: 'sha256=' prefix tolerance to mixed case, missing prefix, double prefix
- Generic HMAC: empty signature, signature longer than expected hash output (truncation must reject, not silently accept matching prefix)
- Timestamp header with negative value, exponent notation, leading zeros, integer overflow on `now - timestamp`

priority rationale: P2. Webhook signature parsers are the SINGLE auth boundary for webhook deliveries; a parse panic kills the webhook handler thread and forces a retry.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-o9niw","title":"[review-pass] Fix telemetry header dedupe and SDK retry counter saturation","description":"# Why
Peer-review follow-on fixes for two concrete correctness bugs introduced or left incomplete in recent review patches.

## Findings
- `crates/fcp-telemetry/src/tracing_layer.rs`: case-insensitive header cleanup removes only one stale case-variant entry, so duplicate `traceparent`/`tracestate` variants can survive injection and later win extraction nondeterministically.
- `crates/fcp-sdk/src/retry.rs` + `crates/fcp-sdk/src/migration.rs`: unlimited retry config (`max_retries = u32::MAX`) maps to `max_attempts = None`, but the retry loop still uses a saturating `u32` attempt counter, which can repeat `operation(u32::MAX)` forever after counter saturation.

## Plan
- remove all stale case-variant tracing headers before injecting fresh context and add focused regressions
- prevent retry scheduling once the attempt counter reaches the representable limit and add focused regressions
- verify with focused `rch` runs only","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:32:21.535447Z","created_by":"SandyStream","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:49:39.520176Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T00:49:39.519789Z","close_reason":"Completed: fixed telemetry context drop leak, removed all stale case-variant trace headers before injection, and stopped SDK unlimited retries at the u32 attempt ceiling","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ofw","title":"fcp.gmail: Google Gmail API Connector","description":"# fcp.gmail - Gmail API Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant Gmail connector for email automation with strict zone isolation, sandboxing, and mechanical authorization.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `operational`
- `streaming` (optional: watch/push or polling-driven event stream)

## Zone model

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The *"zones"* described in this section are a **recommended deployment pattern** (run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies) and/or conceptual risk tiers.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

- Single-zone bound: one connector instance ↔ one `ZoneId`.
- Recommended: `z:private:gmail:<account>` (policy-owned naming).

## External surface
### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only the Gmail/Google API endpoints required by:
- Gmail API
- OAuth token refresh (if used by the OAuth library)

### Credential injection
- OAuth2 refresh/access tokens via `CredentialId`.
- Never persist tokens to disk.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `gmail.messages.read`
- `gmail.messages.send`
- `gmail.messages.modify` (labels/trash)
- `gmail.threads.read` / `gmail.threads.modify`
- `gmail.labels.manage`
- `gmail.settings.manage` (Dangerous)

## Operations (representative)
- messages: get/list/send/reply/forward/modify/trash/delete
- threads: get/list/modify/trash
- labels: list/create/update/delete
- drafts: create/get/list/update/send/delete

## Eventing (optional)
If we support push/watch:
- connector must not expose inbound ports directly
- integrate through `fcp-host` controlled ingress (or deterministic polling)

## State model
- Stateless for simple reads.
- For incremental sync (historyId), persist cursor in connector state and advance under a singleton-writer lease.

## Tests
- Mock-only unit/integration tests with wiremock.
- Deterministic historyId/poll simulation.
- E2E compliance run validates default deny + NetworkConstraints + state/cursor behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner.
- Deterministic tests; no live Gmail calls in unit tests.
